test_cases.description: 3-part series — Arctic fishing communities — Norway to Canadian broadcaster, majority/minority structure
test_cases.input_summary: Norwegian majority production, Canadian storyline in series, 20% co-pro ask, NRK and NFI already attached, specific rights and editorial terms provided
test_cases.output_summary: Brief explains Canadian story necessity (not just financing convenience), deal terms in bullet list, financing architecture shows gap clearly, closes with specific market meeting request

test_cases.description: Single film — urban architecture — UK production approaching German broadcaster
test_cases.input_summary: 90-min film, Berlin and London as dual focus cities, UK as majority producer, Channel 4 attached, approaching ARD/ZDF equivalent as minority partner
test_cases.output_summary: Brief leads with dual-city story structure to establish German relevance, editorial involvement offer limited to German content approval, rights split by territory

test_cases.description: Co-development brief — early-stage project, no financing yet, Australian producer approaching French partner
test_cases.input_summary: Project in concept stage, no financing in place, Australian/French historical story, seeking co-development partner before production deal
test_cases.output_summary: Brief clearly scoped to co-development (not production co-pro), honest about early stage, proposes joint development workshop and shared rights to be structured after development phase

notes: Professionalism is the strongest dimension — the business-tone writing and deal-term formatting are appropriate for international co-production conversations. The skill correctly distinguishes between co-development and co-production stages when inputs are specific about the project's current stage.
